title: linaria striata var. repens
intro: Linaria striata var. repens, also known as creeping toadflax, is a perennial plant species in the toadflax family (Scrophulariaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and pain, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and wound-healing properties. The herbal preparations of creeping toadflax typically involve infusions and decoctions of the plant's flowers and leaves, which can be consumed as teas or applied topically to the skin for its medicinal effects.
